<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0835|2|5}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|children={{ill|Kūkai|WD=Q222227}}; {{ill|Saeki no Suzukimaro|WD=Q11382909}}|father={{ill|Q11450750|WD=Q11450750}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q27917346]] Wikidata name: Tamayorigozen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2021st year 4月13日 (月) 21:14 (UTC)}} '''Tamayori Gozen''' (玉依御前, 生年不明 - [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Shōwa]] 2) was a woman from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. She was the wife of Saeki no Ata, the mother of [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], and the second son, while her eldest son was {{ill|Saeki no Suzukimaro|ja|佐伯鈴伎麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Suzukimaro|1=Saeki no Suzukimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382909}}.
Her name is also referred to as "Akoya Gozen" (阿古屋御前) in some accounts<ref>『続日本後紀』承和3rd year （836年）2月紀。</ref>, but the exact details remain unknown. Her birth year is uncertain, and there are multiple theories about her death year, such as the record in the Mandaraji temple’s history stating that Kūkai prayed for his deceased mother’s soul in Daidō 2 (807), the year following his return from Tang China, or that she passed away on February 5, Shōwa 2 (835).
== Life == One theory suggests that Tamayori Gozen was the daughter of {{ill|Ato no Sukune|ja|安斗智徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Sukune|qq=}}, with a brother named {{ill|Ato no Ōtari|ja|阿刀大足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Ōtari|qq=}}, who served as a tutor to {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. Her family is believed to have been influential and resided near the current location of {{ill|Butsumoin|ja|仏母院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumoin|qq=}}, a short distance from [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]. It is said that she prayed at {{ill|Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja|熊手八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before conceiving Kūkai.
The Butsumoin, also known as Hachiman-san Sankaku-ji Butsumoin, was said to have been established by Kūkai upon his return from Tang China. The temple became the head temple of Kumade Hachiman Shrine<ref>四国「弘法大師の霊跡」巡り 224ページ 川崎一洋・著 2012nd year 12月18日発行 セルバ出版を参照</ref>. Artifacts such as Kūkai’s umbilical cord stored in the "Enazuka" mound and a mud Buddha sculpted by Kūkai as a child still exist.
Another location proposed as her birthplace is near the {{ill|Kaigan-ji Temple|ja|海岸寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan-ji Temple|1=Kaigan-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558882}} Mieidō Hall, where a birthing well is located about 2 km west of Butsumoin. Other claims suggest a birthing well near the Mieidō Hall at Zentsū-ji. Academic perspectives propose that the Ato clan lived in the Kinai region, such as Settsu or Kawachi, and that Kūkai was born and raised in Kinai.
<gallery> File:Tamayoriɡozen.jpg|Statue of Tamayori Gozen File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɾ.jpg|Shishi-no-Iwaya at Mitani-dera File:Tamayoriɡozenœɨ.jpg|Mother's Hall at Butsumoin File:Tamayorigozen02.jpg|Haiden at Jison-in File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɜ.jpg|Front of the Mieidō at Zentsū-ji File:Butimoin 01.jpg|Birthing well at Butsumoin File:Kaiɡanziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Kaigan-ji File:Zentuziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Zentsū-ji </gallery>

Her name is also referred to as "Akoya Gozen" (阿古屋御前) in some accounts<ref>『続日本後紀』承和3rd year （836年）2月紀。</ref>, but the exact details remain unknown. Her birth year is uncertain, and there are multiple theories about her death year, such as the record in the Mandaraji temple’s history stating that Kūkai prayed for his deceased mother’s soul in Daidō 2 (807), the year following his return from Tang China, or that she passed away on February 5, Shōwa 2 (835).
== Life == One theory suggests that Tamayori Gozen was the daughter of {{ill|Ato no Sukune|ja|安斗智徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Sukune|qq=}}, with a brother named {{ill|Ato no Ōtari|ja|阿刀大足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Ōtari|qq=}}, who served as a tutor to {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. Her family is believed to have been influential and resided near the current location of {{ill|Butsumoin|ja|仏母院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumoin|qq=}}, a short distance from [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]. It is said that she prayed at {{ill|Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja|熊手八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before conceiving Kūkai.
The Butsumoin, also known as Hachiman-san Sankaku-ji Butsumoin, was said to have been established by Kūkai upon his return from Tang China. The temple became the head temple of Kumade Hachiman Shrine<ref>四国「弘法大師の霊跡」巡り 224ページ 川崎一洋・著 2012nd year 12月18日発行 セルバ出版を参照</ref>. Artifacts such as Kūkai’s umbilical cord stored in the "Enazuka" mound and a mud Buddha sculpted by Kūkai as a child still exist.
Another location proposed as her birthplace is near the {{ill|Kaigan-ji Temple|ja|海岸寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan-ji Temple|1=Kaigan-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558882}} Mieidō Hall, where a birthing well is located about 2 km west of Butsumoin. Other claims suggest a birthing well near the Mieidō Hall at Zentsū-ji. Academic perspectives propose that the Ato clan lived in the Kinai region, such as Settsu or Kawachi, and that Kūkai was born and raised in Kinai.
<gallery> File:Tamayoriɡozen.jpg|Statue of Tamayori Gozen File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɾ.jpg|Shishi-no-Iwaya at Mitani-dera File:Tamayoriɡozenœɨ.jpg|Mother's Hall at Butsumoin File:Tamayorigozen02.jpg|Haiden at Jison-in File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɜ.jpg|Front of the Mieidō at Zentsū-ji File:Butimoin 01.jpg|Birthing well at Butsumoin File:Kaiɡanziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Kaigan-ji File:Zentuziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Zentsū-ji </gallery>

Academic perspectives propose that the Ato clan lived in the Kinai region, such as Settsu or Kawachi, and that Kūkai was born and raised in Kinai.

<!--Tamayori Gozen--><!--Tamayori Gozen--><!--Tamayori Gozen--><!--Tamayori Gozen--><!--Tamayori Gozen--><!--Tamayori Gozen--><!--Tamayori Gozen--><!--Tamayori Gozen--> [[Category:Saeki clan]] [[Category:Women of the Nara period]] [[Category:Women of the Heian period]] [[Category:Ato clan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Tamayori Gozen}} {{wikidata link|Q27917346}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0835|2|5}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|children={{ill|Kūkai|WD=Q222227}}; {{ill|Saeki no Suzukimaro|WD=Q11382909}}|father={{ill|Q11450750|WD=Q11450750}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q27917346]] Wikidata name: Tamayorigozen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2021st year 4月13日 (月) 21:14 (UTC)}} '''Tamayori Gozen''' (玉依御前, 生年不明 - [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Shōwa]] 2) was a woman from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. She was the wife of Saeki no Ata, the mother of [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], and the second son, while her eldest son was {{ill|Saeki no Suzukimaro|ja|佐伯鈴伎麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Suzukimaro|1=Saeki no Suzukimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382909}}.
Her name is also referred to as "Akoya Gozen" (阿古屋御前) in some accounts<ref>『続日本後紀』承和3rd year （836年）2月紀。</ref>, but the exact details remain unknown. Her birth year is uncertain, and there are multiple theories about her death year, such as the record in the Mandaraji temple’s history stating that Kūkai prayed for his deceased mother’s soul in Daidō 2 (807), the year following his return from Tang China, or that she passed away on February 5, Shōwa 2 (835).
== Life == One theory suggests that Tamayori Gozen was the daughter of {{ill|Ato no Sukune|ja|安斗智徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Sukune|qq=}}, with a brother named {{ill|Ato no Ōtari|ja|阿刀大足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Ōtari|qq=}}, who served as a tutor to {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. Her family is believed to have been influential and resided near the current location of {{ill|Butsumoin|ja|仏母院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumoin|qq=}}, a short distance from [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]. It is said that she prayed at {{ill|Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja|熊手八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before conceiving Kūkai.
The Butsumoin, also known as Hachiman-san Sankaku-ji Butsumoin, was said to have been established by Kūkai upon his return from Tang China. The temple became the head temple of Kumade Hachiman Shrine<ref>四国「弘法大師の霊跡」巡り 224ページ 川崎一洋・著 2012nd year 12月18日発行 セルバ出版を参照</ref>. Artifacts such as Kūkai’s umbilical cord stored in the "Enazuka" mound and a mud Buddha sculpted by Kūkai as a child still exist.
Another location proposed as her birthplace is near the {{ill|Kaigan-ji Temple|ja|海岸寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan-ji Temple|1=Kaigan-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558882}} Mieidō Hall, where a birthing well is located about 2 km west of Butsumoin. Other claims suggest a birthing well near the Mieidō Hall at Zentsū-ji. Academic perspectives propose that the Ato clan lived in the Kinai region, such as Settsu or Kawachi, and that Kūkai was born and raised in Kinai.
<gallery> File:Tamayoriɡozen.jpg|Statue of Tamayori Gozen File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɾ.jpg|Shishi-no-Iwaya at Mitani-dera File:Tamayoriɡozenœɨ.jpg|Mother's Hall at Butsumoin File:Tamayorigozen02.jpg|Haiden at Jison-in File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɜ.jpg|Front of the Mieidō at Zentsū-ji File:Butimoin 01.jpg|Birthing well at Butsumoin File:Kaiɡanziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Kaigan-ji File:Zentuziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Zentsū-ji </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0835|2|5}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|children={{ill|Kūkai|WD=Q222227}}; {{ill|Saeki no Suzukimaro|WD=Q11382909}}|father={{ill|Q11450750|WD=Q11450750}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q27917346]] Wikidata name: Tamayorigozen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2021st year 4月13日 (月) 21:14 (UTC)}} '''Tamayori Gozen''' (玉依御前, 生年不明 - [[:en:Jōwa (Heian period)|Shōwa]] 2) was a woman from the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] to the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]]. She was the wife of Saeki no Ata, the mother of [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]], and the second son, while her eldest son was {{ill|Saeki no Suzukimaro|ja|佐伯鈴伎麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Suzukimaro|1=Saeki no Suzukimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382909}}.
Her name is also referred to as "Akoya Gozen" (阿古屋御前) in some accounts<ref>『続日本後紀』承和3rd year （836年）2月紀。</ref>, but the exact details remain unknown. Her birth year is uncertain, and there are multiple theories about her death year, such as the record in the Mandaraji temple’s history stating that Kūkai prayed for his deceased mother’s soul in Daidō 2 (807), the year following his return from Tang China, or that she passed away on February 5, Shōwa 2 (835).
== Life == One theory suggests that Tamayori Gozen was the daughter of {{ill|Ato no Sukune|ja|安斗智徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Sukune|qq=}}, with a brother named {{ill|Ato no Ōtari|ja|阿刀大足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Ōtari|qq=}}, who served as a tutor to {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. Her family is believed to have been influential and resided near the current location of {{ill|Butsumoin|ja|仏母院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumoin|qq=}}, a short distance from [[:en:Zentsū-ji|Zentsū-ji]]. It is said that she prayed at {{ill|Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja|熊手八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before conceiving Kūkai.
The Butsumoin, also known as Hachiman-san Sankaku-ji Butsumoin, was said to have been established by Kūkai upon his return from Tang China. The temple became the head temple of Kumade Hachiman Shrine<ref>四国「弘法大師の霊跡」巡り 224ページ 川崎一洋・著 2012nd year 12月18日発行 セルバ出版を参照</ref>. Artifacts such as Kūkai’s umbilical cord stored in the "Enazuka" mound and a mud Buddha sculpted by Kūkai as a child still exist.
Another location proposed as her birthplace is near the {{ill|Kaigan-ji Temple|ja|海岸寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan-ji Temple|1=Kaigan-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558882}} Mieidō Hall, where a birthing well is located about 2 km west of Butsumoin. Other claims suggest a birthing well near the Mieidō Hall at Zentsū-ji. Academic perspectives propose that the Ato clan lived in the Kinai region, such as Settsu or Kawachi, and that Kūkai was born and raised in Kinai.
<gallery> File:Tamayoriɡozen.jpg|Statue of Tamayori Gozen File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɾ.jpg|Shishi-no-Iwaya at Mitani-dera File:Tamayoriɡozenœɨ.jpg|Mother's Hall at Butsumoin File:Tamayorigozen02.jpg|Haiden at Jison-in File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɜ.jpg|Front of the Mieidō at Zentsū-ji File:Butimoin 01.jpg|Birthing well at Butsumoin File:Kaiɡanziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Kaigan-ji File:Zentuziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Zentsū-ji </gallery>

It is said that she prayed at {{ill|Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja|熊手八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before conceiving Kūkai.
The Butsumoin, also known as Hachiman-san Sankaku-ji Butsumoin, was said to have been established by Kūkai upon his return from Tang China. The temple became the head temple of Kumade Hachiman Shrine<ref>四国「弘法大師の霊跡」巡り 224ページ 川崎一洋・著 2012nd year 12月18日発行 セルバ出版を参照</ref>. Artifacts such as Kūkai’s umbilical cord stored in the "Enazuka" mound and a mud Buddha sculpted by Kūkai as a child still exist.
Another location proposed as her birthplace is near the {{ill|Kaigan-ji Temple|ja|海岸寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan-ji Temple|1=Kaigan-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558882}} Mieidō Hall, where a birthing well is located about 2 km west of Butsumoin. Other claims suggest a birthing well near the Mieidō Hall at Zentsū-ji. Academic perspectives propose that the Ato clan lived in the Kinai region, such as Settsu or Kawachi, and that Kūkai was born and raised in Kinai.
<gallery> File:Tamayoriɡozen.jpg|Statue of Tamayori Gozen File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɾ.jpg|Shishi-no-Iwaya at Mitani-dera File:Tamayoriɡozenœɨ.jpg|Mother's Hall at Butsumoin File:Tamayorigozen02.jpg|Haiden at Jison-in File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɜ.jpg|Front of the Mieidō at Zentsū-ji File:Butimoin 01.jpg|Birthing well at Butsumoin File:Kaiɡanziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Kaigan-ji File:Zentuziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Zentsū-ji </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0835|2|5}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|children={{ill|Kūkai|WD=Q222227}}; {{ill|Saeki no Suzukimaro|WD=Q11382909}}|father={{ill|Q11450750|WD=Q11450750}}}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2021st year 4月13日 (月) 21:14 (UTC)}} '''Tamayori Gozen''' (玉依御前, 生年不明 - {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Shōwa}} 2) was a woman from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. She was the wife of Saeki no Ata, the mother of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}}, and the second son, while her eldest son was {{ill|Saeki no Suzukimaro|ja|佐伯鈴伎麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Suzukimaro|1=Saeki no Suzukimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382909}}.
Her name is also referred to as "Akoya Gozen" (阿古屋御前) in some accounts<ref>『続日本後紀』承和3rd year （836年）2月紀。</ref>, but the exact details remain unknown. Her birth year is uncertain, and there are multiple theories about her death year, such as the record in the Mandaraji temple’s history stating that Kūkai prayed for his deceased mother’s soul in Daidō 2 (807), the year following his return from Tang China, or that she passed away on February 5, Shōwa 2 (835).
== Life == One theory suggests that Tamayori Gozen was the daughter of {{ill|Ato no Sukune|ja|安斗智徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Sukune|qq=}}, with a brother named {{ill|Ato no Ōtari|ja|阿刀大足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Ōtari|qq=}}, who served as a tutor to {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. Her family is believed to have been influential and resided near the current location of {{ill|Butsumoin|ja|仏母院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumoin|qq=}}, a short distance from {{ill|Zentsū-ji|en|Zentsū-ji|WD=Q787035|lt=Zentsū-ji}}. It is said that she prayed at {{ill|Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja|熊手八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before conceiving Kūkai.
The Butsumoin, also known as Hachiman-san Sankaku-ji Butsumoin, was said to have been established by Kūkai upon his return from Tang China. The temple became the head temple of Kumade Hachiman Shrine<ref>四国「弘法大師の霊跡」巡り 224ページ 川崎一洋・著 2012nd year 12月18日発行 セルバ出版を参照</ref>. Artifacts such as Kūkai’s umbilical cord stored in the "Enazuka" mound and a mud Buddha sculpted by Kūkai as a child still exist.
Another location proposed as her birthplace is near the {{ill|Kaigan-ji Temple|ja|海岸寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan-ji Temple|1=Kaigan-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558882}} Mieidō Hall, where a birthing well is located about 2 km west of Butsumoin. Other claims suggest a birthing well near the Mieidō Hall at Zentsū-ji. Academic perspectives propose that the Ato clan lived in the Kinai region, such as Settsu or Kawachi, and that Kūkai was born and raised in Kinai.
<gallery> File:Tamayoriɡozen.jpg|Statue of Tamayori Gozen File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɾ.jpg|Shishi-no-Iwaya at Mitani-dera File:Tamayoriɡozenœɨ.jpg|Mother's Hall at Butsumoin File:Tamayorigozen02.jpg|Haiden at Jison-in File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɜ.jpg|Front of the Mieidō at Zentsū-ji File:Butimoin 01.jpg|Birthing well at Butsumoin File:Kaiɡanziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Kaigan-ji File:Zentuziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Zentsū-ji </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tamayori Gozen]][[ja:玉依御前]] {{Infobox person|death_date={{death date|0835|2|5}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|children={{ill|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}; {{ill|Saeki no Suzukimaro|WD=Q11382909|1=Saeki no Suzukimaro}}|father={{ill|Q11450750|WD=Q11450750|1=Ato no Sukune}}}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2021st year 4月13日 (月) 21:14 (UTC)}} '''Tamayori Gozen''' (玉依御前, 生年不明 - {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|WD=Q1075605|lt=Shōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} 2) was a woman from the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} to the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. She was the wife of Saeki no Ata, the mother of {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}}, and the second son, while her eldest son was {{ill|Saeki no Suzukimaro|ja|佐伯鈴伎麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Suzukimaro|1=Saeki no Suzukimaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11382909}}.
Her name is also referred to as "Akoya Gozen" (阿古屋御前) in some accounts<ref>『続日本後紀』承和3rd year （836年）2月紀。</ref>, but the exact details remain unknown. Her birth year is uncertain, and there are multiple theories about her death year, such as the record in the Mandaraji temple’s history stating that Kūkai prayed for his deceased mother’s soul in Daidō 2 (807), the year following his return from Tang China, or that she passed away on February 5, Shōwa 2 (835).
== Life == One theory suggests that Tamayori Gozen was the daughter of {{ill|Ato no Sukune|ja|安斗智徳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Sukune|qq=}}, with a brother named {{ill|Ato no Ōtari|ja|阿刀大足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Ōtari|qq=}}, who served as a tutor to {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. Her family is believed to have been influential and resided near the current location of {{ill|Butsumoin|ja|仏母院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Butsumoin|qq=}}, a short distance from {{ill|Zentsū-ji|en|Zentsū-ji|WD=Q787035|lt=Zentsū-ji|1=Zentsū-ji}}. It is said that she prayed at {{ill|Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja|熊手八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumade Hachiman Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} before conceiving Kūkai.
The Butsumoin, also known as Hachiman-san Sankaku-ji Butsumoin, was said to have been established by Kūkai upon his return from Tang China. The temple became the head temple of Kumade Hachiman Shrine<ref>四国「弘法大師の霊跡」巡り 224ページ 川崎一洋・著 2012nd year 12月18日発行 セルバ出版を参照</ref>. Artifacts such as Kūkai’s umbilical cord stored in the "Enazuka" mound and a mud Buddha sculpted by Kūkai as a child still exist.
Another location proposed as her birthplace is near the {{ill|Kaigan-ji Temple|ja|海岸寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaigan-ji Temple|1=Q11558882|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11558882}} Mieidō Hall, where a birthing well is located about 2 km west of Butsumoin. Other claims suggest a birthing well near the Mieidō Hall at Zentsū-ji. Academic perspectives propose that the Ato clan lived in the Kinai region, such as Settsu or Kawachi, and that Kūkai was born and raised in Kinai.
<gallery> File:Tamayoriɡozen.jpg|Statue of Tamayori Gozen File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɾ.jpg|Shishi-no-Iwaya at Mitani-dera File:Tamayoriɡozenœɨ.jpg|Mother's Hall at Butsumoin File:Tamayorigozen02.jpg|Haiden at Jison-in File:Tamayoriɡozen0ɜ.jpg|Front of the Mieidō at Zentsū-ji File:Butimoin 01.jpg|Birthing well at Butsumoin File:Kaiɡanziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Kaigan-ji File:Zentuziøɨ.jpg|Birthing well at Zentsū-ji </gallery>
